title: Unit Fractions of a Whole
essential_question: How can you use a fraction to name one part of a whole that is divided into equal parts?
strategies: identify the number of equal parts (denominator)
the numerator of a unit fraction is always 1

pretest.question: A circle is divided into 3 equal parts. What unit fraction names each part?
pretest.explanation: 3 equal parts → each part is the unit fraction 1/3.

pretest.question: Which is a unit fraction?
pretest.explanation: A unit fraction has a numerator of 1. Only 1/4 has a numerator of 1.

pretest.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. 1 part is shaded. What fraction is shaded?
pretest.explanation: 1 shaded part out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.

pretest.question: In the fraction 1/8, what does the 8 tell you?
choices: The number of equal parts in the whole
pretest.explanation: The denominator (8) tells the number of equal parts the whole is divided into.

pretest.question: Which unit fraction names the largest part of a whole?
pretest.explanation: The fewer equal parts, the larger each part. 1/2 is the largest because the whole is split into only 2 parts.

learn.title: What Is a Unit Fraction?
learn.content: A unit fraction names 1 equal part of a whole. It always has 1 as the numerator. Examples: 1/2, 1/3, 1/4, 1/6, 1/8.

learn.title: Numerator and Denominator
learn.content: Every fraction has two numbers. The top number is the numerator — it tells how many parts you are talking about. The bottom number is the denominator — it tells how many equal parts are in the whole. In 1/4, the numerator is 1 and the denominator is 4.

learn.title: Smaller Denominator = Larger Part
learn.content: When a whole is divided into fewer parts, each part is bigger. So 1/2 is larger than 1/4, and 1/4 is larger than 1/8.

try.question: A square is divided into 4 equal parts. What unit fraction names each part?
Which is NOT a unit fraction?
try.explanation: 2/3 has a numerator of 2, not 1, so it is not a unit fraction.

practice_r1.question: A pie is divided into 8 equal slices. What unit fraction names each slice?
A strip of paper is folded into 2 equal parts. What fraction is each part?
In the fraction 1/6, what is the denominator?
practice_r1.explanation: The denominator is the bottom number. In 1/6, the denominator is 6.

practice_r1.question: A hexagon is divided into 6 equal parts. 1 part is colored. What fraction is colored?
Which is a unit fraction?
practice_r1.explanation: 1/3 has 1 as the numerator, so it is a unit fraction.

practice_r1.question: A rectangle is divided into 3 equal parts. What unit fraction names each part?
In the fraction 1/4, what does the 1 tell you?
choices: There is 1 part being talked about
practice_r1.explanation: The numerator (1) tells how many parts you are talking about.

practice_r2.question: A whole is divided into equal parts. Each part is 1/8. How many equal parts are there?
practice_r2.explanation: If each part is 1/8, the whole is divided into 8 equal parts.

practice_r2.question: Which unit fraction names the smallest part of a whole?
practice_r2.explanation: More equal parts means each part is smaller. 1/8 is the smallest because the whole is split into 8 parts.

practice_r2.question: Mia says 1/6 is bigger than 1/4 because 6 > 4. Is she correct?
choices: Yes, bigger denominator means bigger fraction
No, bigger denominator means smaller parts
No, they are the same size
practice_r2.explanation: A bigger denominator means more parts, so each part is smaller. 1/6 < 1/4.

practice_r2.question: A shape is divided into equal parts. One part is shaded and it represents 1/3 of the whole. How many parts does the shape have in all?
practice_r2.explanation: 1/3 means 1 out of 3 equal parts, so the shape has 3 parts in all.

practice_r3.question: Sam has a ribbon. He cuts it so each piece is 1/4 of the whole ribbon. Then he cuts each piece in half. What unit fraction names each smaller piece?
practice_r3.explanation: 1/4 cut in half = 1/8. The ribbon now has 4 × 2 = 8 equal pieces.

practice_r3.question: Which statement is true?
practice_r3.explanation: Fewer parts = larger pieces. 1/2 (2 parts) is greater than 1/6 (6 parts).

practice_r3.question: A pizza is cut into equal slices. Each slice is bigger than 1/6, but smaller than 1/3. What could the unit fraction for each slice be?
practice_r3.explanation: 1/4 is between 1/3 and 1/6 in size (1/3 > 1/4 > 1/6), so 1/4 works.
